titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Winant ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Winant ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 3002. Comea 196. In conversation tonight with Mosely, De Leusse,Pierre Marie de Leusse, Second Counselor of the French Embassy in London Massigli’s assistant on the EAC, put forward informally and not as an official proposal the following division of districts in greater Berlin. De Leusse discussed it today with HoodViscount Samuel Hood, member of the British Delegation to the European Advisory Commission of the Foreign Office and apparently has not presented it to the Soviet delegation.
The informal French proposal is as follows:
Kreutzberg, Schoeneberg, Steglitz, Neukölln, Tempelhof, Treptow.
Weissensee, Prenzlauerberg, Mitte, Friedrichshain, Lichtenberg, Köpenick.

Wedding, Pankow, Reinickendorf, Spandau. De Leusse indicated that Hood’s reaction was that the proposed British area was not adequate in terms of facilities; De Leusse thought Charlottenburg might be shifted from the French to the British area. Obviously the French are not satisfied with the area proposed by the British for French occupation (my 2092, February 28, 10 p.m., Comea 189) and are seeking some adjustment which would give them better railroad and hotel facilities.
